summary: American playwright, Nobel laureate (1936). Most commonly identified by Long Day's Journey into Night (Tyrone family, Mary's morphine addiction, Edmund's tuberculosis, fog motif), The Iceman Cometh (Harry Hope's saloon, Hickey, pipe dreams), The Emperor Jones (Brutus Jones, silver bullet, drums), The Hairy Ape (Yank, Mildred Douglas, 'filthy beast,' gorilla), and Anna Christie (Mat Burke, Chris Christopherson, prostitution). Founded with Provincetown Players by Susan Glaspell. Father James O'Neill starred in Count of Monte Cristo 6,000+ times. Daughter Oona married Charlie Chaplin.

works.name: Long Day's Journey into Night
works.description: O'Neill's autobiographical masterwork about the dysfunctional Tyrone family over one long day. Mary Tyrone is addicted to morphine; Edmund has tuberculosis; the actor James is dissatisfied with having played one role (Count of Monte Cristo) throughout his career; Jamie is an alcoholic. Fog and foghorn are recurring motifs. Mary complains about the foghorn and reminisces about her youth in a convent, holding her wedding dress at the end. The father and son argue about keeping a lightbulb on. Edmund quotes Swinburne and wishes he'd been born a 'seagull or a fish.' Mary plays piano at the very end despite maimed fingers. Leon Edel biography volumes.

clues.clue: Tyrone family: Mary (morphine addict), James (actor), Jamie (alcoholic), Edmund (tuberculosis)
examples: the tuberculosis-afflicted Edmund and his morphine-addicted mother Mary are members of the Tyrone family
the alcoholic, cynical actor Jamie and the morphine addicted matriarch Mary

clues.clue: Fog and foghorn motif; Mary describes love of fog; complaints about foghorn
examples: a character's deteriorating mental state is represented by her wedding dress and the appearance of fog

clues.clue: Mary ends play holding wedding dress; plays piano despite maimed fingers
examples: a woman reminiscing on her youth in a convent as she holds her coveted wedding dress

clues.clue: Father and son argue about lightbulb; James berates Edmund's taste in literature (Swinburne)
examples: two characters argue about keeping a lightbulb on before the father berates the son's taste in literature

clues.clue: James played Count of Monte Cristo 6,000+ times; based on O'Neill's real father
examples: the actor James is dissatisfied with having played one role throughout his career

works.name: The Iceman Cometh
works.description: Set in Harry Hope's saloon, where regulars cling to their 'pipe dreams.' The salesman Theodore Hickman (Hickey) confesses to murdering his wife Evelyn. Don Parritt jumps off a fire escape. Hugo declaims 'The days grow hot, O Babylon!' Rocky denies being a pimp. Joe Mott rails against racial abuse. The play marked O'Neill's return to Broadway after 12 years.

clues.clue: Harry Hope's saloon; Hickey confesses to murdering wife Evelyn; pipe dreams
examples: Theodore Hickman confesses to the murder of his wife to the patrons of Harry Hope's saloon

clues.clue: Don Parritt commits suicide by jumping off fire escape
examples: Don Parrit jumps off a fire escape after Hickey reveals that he killed his wife

clues.clue: Hugo declaims 'The days grow hot, O Babylon!'; 'capitalist svine' and 'bourgeois monkeys'
examples: 'The days grow hot, O Babylon!'
decrying 'capitalist svine' and 'bourgeois monkeys'

examples: Rocky Pioggi vehemently denies that he's a pimp

works.name: The Emperor Jones
works.description: Brutus Jones, a former Pullman porter, establishes himself as ruler of a Caribbean island and claims he can only be killed by a silver bullet. A drum starts beating midway and steadily accelerates. Jones stumbles through a forest seeing Little Formless Fears, a slave auction, and the ghost of the murdered Jeff. Smithers advises him to flee. Lem leads a rebellion. Paul Robeson starred.

clues.clue: Brutus Jones claims only silver bullet can kill him; rules Caribbean island; drums accelerate throughout
examples: a Pullman porter who claims he can only be killed by a silver bullet while ruling a Caribbean island
a drum starts beating and steadily increases in speed

clues.clue: Little Formless Fears emerge from forest; slave auction hallucination; ghost of Jeff
examples: a man murdered for cheating at dice is one of the 'little formless fears'
fails to find a tin box of food he has hidden

clues.clue: Paul Robeson starred; Smithers advises Jones to flee
examples: Paul Robeson, who also played a Pullman porter
Smithers advises the title character to flee from an island

works.name: The Hairy Ape
works.description: Yank (Bob Smith), a ship's coal stoker, is called 'oh, the filthy beast!' by the steel heiress Mildred Douglas. He adopts the pose of Rodin's The Thinker and tries to join the IWW but is kicked out for proposing to blow up a steel factory. He passes rich pedestrians wearing masks on Fifth Avenue. Ends when a gorilla crushes Yank at the zoo; stage direction notes he 'at last belongs.'

clues.clue: Mildred Douglas calls Yank 'filthy beast'; Yank poses as Rodin's Thinker; crushed by gorilla at zoo
examples: Mildred Douglas exclaims 'oh, the filthy beast!' upon seeing the coal stoker Yank

clues.clue: Yank kicked out of IWW for proposing to blow up steel; stage direction 'at last belongs'
examples: The protagonist is kicked out of the IWW after proposing to blow up a steel factory

clues.clue: First four scenes on ship; 'Drink! Don't think!' chant by firemen
examples: off-duty firemen chant, 'Drink! Don't think!'

works.name: Anna Christie
works.description: Anna Christie, a former prostitute, defies her estranged Swedish father Chris Christopherson by eloping with the shipwreck survivor Mat Burke. Anna confesses her past to both Burke and her father. First act set in Johnny the Priest's saloon. The 1930 film had the tagline 'Garbo talks!' S.S. Glencairn plays are related one-act works.

clues.clue: Anna confesses past as prostitute to Mat Burke and father Chris Christopherson
examples: a character claims to hate all men while telling her lover Burke and her father Chris Christopherson that she is a prostitute

clues.clue: First act in Johnny the Priest's saloon; 'dat ole davil sea'
examples: Longshoremen order drinks from Johnny the Priest's bar

clues.clue: 1930 film: 'Garbo talks!' tagline
examples: A film with the tagline 'Garbo talks!' adapts a play by this author

works.name: Other Works and Biographical
works.description: Additional works: Strange Interlude (Nina Leeds, nine acts, lengthy soliloquies/asides to audience; Sam Evans; Charles Marsden; Nina watches Gordon's plane at end), A Moon for the Misbegotten (sequel to LDJIN; Jamie Tyrone and Josie; Phil Hogan's land), Ah Wilderness! (O'Neill's only comedy; Richard Miller courts Muriel; July 4th), Mourning Becomes Electra (retelling of Oresteia with Mannon family), Beyond the Horizon (Robert dies of tuberculosis), Desire Under the Elms (Abbie Putnam kills child), All God's Chillun Got Wings (interracial Jim and Ella; Paul Robeson), The Great God Brown (masks), Dynamo (Reuben Light: 'No God but Electricity!'). Provincetown Players with Susan Glaspell. Father James O'Neill played Count of Monte Cristo 6,000+ times. Daughter Oona married Charlie Chaplin; was disowned. Nobel Prize mention of debt to Strindberg.

clues.clue: Strange Interlude: Nina Leeds; nine acts; stream-of-consciousness soliloquies/asides; Sam Evans; Charles Marsden

Strange Interlude frequently features characters engaging in this action

examples: The Provincetown Players premiered many of the early plays by this man

clues.clue: Daughter Oona married Charlie Chaplin; disowned by O'Neill

clues.clue: Nobel acceptance speech: 'too proud of my debt to Strindberg'

clues.clue: The Great God Brown: masks; 'Memoranda on Masks' essay
examples: using these masks in theatre allows for the playwright to 'express those profound hidden conflicts of the mind'

comprehensive_summary:
Eugene O'Neill is the foundational American playwright in quizbowl, appearing across all difficulty levels. Long Day's Journey into Night is his most heavily clued work, centering on the autobiographical Tyrone family: Mary (morphine addict), James (actor who played Count of Monte Cristo 6,000+ times), Jamie (alcoholic), and Edmund (tuberculosis). Fog serves as a central motif. Mary ends the play holding her wedding dress and playing piano despite maimed fingers.

The Iceman Cometh is set in Harry Hope's saloon, where the salesman Hickey confesses to murdering his wife Evelyn and urges regulars to give up their 'pipe dreams.' Don Parritt jumps off a fire escape. The Emperor Jones features Brutus Jones, a former Pullman porter ruling a Caribbean island who claims he can only be killed by a silver bullet, with drums steadily accelerating throughout. Paul Robeson starred. The Hairy Ape features the stoker Yank, called 'filthy beast' by Mildred Douglas, who adopts the pose of Rodin's Thinker and is ultimately crushed by a gorilla at the zoo.

Anna Christie depicts a former prostitute who defies her father Chris Christopherson to marry the shipwreck survivor Mat Burke. Strange Interlude features Nina Leeds and nine acts of lengthy soliloquies. A Moon for the Misbegotten is a sequel to Long Day's Journey featuring Jamie Tyrone. Ah, Wilderness! is O'Neill's only comedy. The Provincetown Players, co-founded with Susan Glaspell, premiered many early works. O'Neill's daughter Oona married Charlie Chaplin. His Nobel speech acknowledged a debt to Strindberg.

cards.front: Play: morphine-addicted Mary, alcoholic Jamie, actor James (6,000 performances of Monte Cristo), and tuberculosis-afflicted Edmund make up a dysfunctional family
cards.back: Long Day's Journey into Night (Eugene O'Neill)

cards.front: Play: ends with mother holding wedding dress and playing piano despite maimed fingers; fog and foghorn are recurring motifs

cards.front: Play: salesman Hickey confesses to murdering wife Evelyn in Harry Hope's saloon; urges regulars to give up their 'pipe dreams'
Play: former Pullman porter rules Caribbean island and claims only a silver bullet can kill him; drums steadily accelerate throughout
Play: coal stoker Yank called 'filthy beast' by steel heiress Mildred Douglas; poses as Rodin's Thinker; crushed by gorilla at zoo
Play: Nina Leeds is the protagonist of a nine-act play with stream-of-consciousness soliloquies delivered as asides to the audience
Play: only comedy by this author; Richard Miller courts Muriel on July 4th; parents encourage him to attend Yale
Playwright: essay 'Memoranda on Masks' argues masks allow playwrights to 'express profound hidden conflicts of the mind'
